title: Differential equations: exponential model equations
description: Solve differential equations that describe exponential relationships. Such equations always have the form dy/dx=ky (for some number k).

hints:
The general solution of equations of the form $\dfrac{dy}{dx}=ky$ is

$y=C\cdot e^{kx}$

for some constant $C$.

This can be found using separation of variables. [[☃ explanation 1]]

In our case, $k=-3$, so $y=C\cdot e^{-3x}$.
In conclusion, $y=2e^{-3x}$.

The general solution of equations of the form $\dfrac{dy}{dt}=ky$ is

$y=C\cdot e^{kt}$

for some constant $C$.

This can be found using separation of variables. [[☃ explanation 1]]

In our case, $k=-7$, so $y=C\cdot e^{-7t}$.
In conclusion, $y=3e^{-7t}$.

The general solution of equations of the form $\dfrac{dy}{dt}=ky$ is

$y=C\cdot e^{kt}$

for some constant $C$.

This can be found using separation of variables. [[☃ explanation 1]]

In our case, $k=2$, so $y=C\cdot e^{2t}$.
In conclusion, $y=8e^{2t}$.

The general solution of equations of the form $\dfrac{dy}{dx}=ky$ is

$y=C\cdot e^{kx}$

for some constant $C$.

This can be found using separation of variables. [[☃ explanation 1]]

In our case, $k=7$, so $y=C\cdot e^{7x}$.
In conclusion, $y=e^{7x}$.

The general solution of equations of the form $f'(x)=kf(x)$ is

$f(x)=C\cdot e^{kx}$

for some constant $C$.

This can be found using separation of variables. [[☃ explanation 1]]

In our case, $k=4$, so $f(x)=C\cdot e^{4x}$.
In conclusion, $f(x)=10e^{4x}$.

The general solution of equations of the form $g'(x)=kg(x)$ is

$g(x)=C\cdot e^{kx}$

for some constant $C$.

This can be found using separation of variables. [[☃ explanation 2]]

In our case, $k=-2$, so $g(x)=C\cdot e^{-2x}$.
In conclusion, $g(x)=5e^{-2x}$.

The general solution of equations of the form $h'(x)=kh(x)$ is

$h(x)=C\cdot e^{kx}$

for some constant $C$.

This can be found using separation of variables. [[☃ explanation 2]]

In our case, $k=-1$, so $h(x)=C\cdot e^{-x}$.
In conclusion, $h(x)=12e^{-x}$.

The general solution of equations of the form $f'(x)=kf(x)$ is

$f(x)=C\cdot e^{kx}$

for some constant $C$.

This can be found using separation of variables. [[☃ explanation 2]]

In our case, $k=-5$, so $f(x)=C\cdot e^{-5x}$.
In conclusion, $f(x)=4e^{-5x}$.

The general solution of equations of the form $\dfrac{dy}{dx}=ky$ is

$y=C\cdot e^{kx}$

for some constant $C$.

This can be found using separation of variables. [[☃ explanation 1]]

In our case, $k=3$, so $y=C\cdot e^{3x}$.
In conclusion, $y=2e^{3x-3}$.

In our case, $k=-4$, so $y=C\cdot e^{-4x}$.
In conclusion, $y=3e^{8-4x}$.

The general solution of equations of the form $\dfrac{dy}{dt}=ky$ is

$y=C\cdot e^{kt}$

for some constant $C$.

This can be found using separation of variables. [[☃ explanation 1]]

In our case, $k=1$, so $y=C\cdot e^{t}$.
In conclusion, $y=e^{t-4}$.

In conclusion, $g(x)=2e^{5x-15}$.
